pi_items.source: Code analysis of benchmarks/cua_world/environments/zotero_env/assets/sample_data/classic_papers.bib contains exactly 10 entries, which is within the range [9, 11]
Code analysis of benchmarks/cua_world/environments/zotero_env/assets/sample_data/classic_papers.bib contains exactly 10 entries, which is within the range [9, 11]
setup_task.sh copies the file from /workspace/assets/sample_data/classic_papers.bib to /home/ga/Documents/classic_papers.bib; file confirmed to exist at source location with 10 entries
Direct inspection of classic_papers.bib file shows all four authors present: Einstein (Einstein1905), Turing (Turing1936), Knuth (Knuth1984), Shannon (Shannon1948)

privileged_info_summary: The classic_papers.bib BibTeX file contains exactly 10 papers: Einstein's 1905 work on electrodynamics, Knuth's TeXbook, Turing's 1936 computable numbers paper, Dijkstra's 1959 graph algorithm note, Shannon's 1948 communication theory paper, Feynman's 1965 QED work, Darwin's Origin of Species, Watson and Crick's 1953 DNA structure paper, von Neumann's 1945 EDVAC report, and Church's 1936 unsolvable problem paper. All four expected authors (Einstein, Turing, Knuth, Shannon) are present in the file. The metadata bounds of 9-11 items correctly encompass the actual 10 entries.
